text: A sharp noise echoes from somewhere far below. It does not repeat.
Footsteps on the stairs — distant, unhurried. They fade before you can place them.
Silence. You stop and listen. Nothing. Not even air moving.
The clomping of your own feet. The poverty of sensation here makes every sound loud.
A voice, very far away. Words indistinguishable. It stops.
The corridor ahead is identical to the one behind. You check. It is.
Identical spines. Identical shelves. Identical carpet. Each person is the only difference in these halls.
You've been here before. Or somewhere exactly like it. There is no difference.
The kiosk hums faintly as you pass a rest area. The only machine sound in the library.
Dust motes drift in the light between shelves. The only motion not your own.
The carpet absorbs everything. Your footsteps, your breathing, your presence.
A faint draft from the chasm side. The closest thing to wind you'll find here.
No animals. No insects. No weather. Nothing alive that isn't a person.
The shelves stretch away in both directions. You cannot see where they end.
The light is steady and even. It never flickers, never changes, never suggests a time of day.
A figure in the distance, silhouetted between the shelves. Moving slowly. They don't look up.
Someone has carved a small shape from soap and placed it carefully on a shelf. A chicken, maybe. Nested in coconut fiber.
A pile of books near the railing, spines cracked, pages fanned. Someone threw them and walked away.
A strip of cloth tied to the railing. Someone's marker. It means nothing to you.
A person sitting against the shelves, knees drawn up, staring at nothing. They don't acknowledge you.
